| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/missouri-billion-dollar-stadium-grift?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

On [four](https://substack.com/redirect/d39fce56-e435-458f-8ed6-01826ba301b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [different](https://substack.com/redirect/8ca83e4f-6c26-4a0f-b711-63aaeff54099?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [occasions](https://substack.com/redirect/e2e46efa-5f43-401c-bd53-34bd4d3768e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [now](https://substack.com/redirect/3faa603c-44c8-46b7-9458-2dfa2f4d441f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I've mentioned in passing that government subsidies for professional sports stadiums are a terrible use of taxpayer money.
But Missouri Governor Mike Kehoe doesn't listen to me,1 and today he called back Missouri lawmakers for a [special legislative session](https://substack.com/redirect/7d84733b-8d06-4863-a35a-7aa8612c014e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to provide [up to $1.5 billion](https://substack.com/redirect/07c96981-9bff-4772-bcb1-3a5be8bedc92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)2 in [subsidies for Kansas City stadiums](https://substack.com/redirect/2ac0a084-db16-4df9-acaa-f749f377d7a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): a new stadium for the Royals, and renovations for the Chiefs.
Missouri should reject this effort. It wastes taxpayer money, provides few tangible benefits, and keeps money from being spent more effectively--which is a low bar, because just about anything would be a more effective use of money.
_For those of you in Missouri:**please forward this email to your friends and to your state legislators** ; they're [voting on this proposal](https://substack.com/redirect/5cf277ce-364a-4e6b-9ed5-14b45f1070bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as we speak. [Here's where you can look up](https://substack.com/redirect/6310b472-11b5-4f35-adea-209b9919dfab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who your state legislators are and how to reach them._
_[Representatives](https://substack.com/redirect/804c941f-b4cf-4b9e-9ef9-77a85424c511?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) use **firstname.lastname@house.mo.gov** and [Senators](https://substack.com/redirect/c4ab44e7-cdbb-4a8b-a5fc-22c64d366461?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) use **firstname.lastname@senate.mo.gov** for their official email addresses._
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTY0ODM3ODQ1LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ4ODc3MTA5LCJleHAiOjE3NTE0NjkxMDksImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.iRW_zF9vIc220r8mFbjujEPyjB6HUxUeRa9FQCPYWMg?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _Kansas Citians already voted down public stadium funding. The evidence is overwhelming that it 's a bad use of taxpayer money._
> * _The jobs being created and supported by the Chiefs are, in reality, less than 10% of what the Missouri Governor 's Office is claiming._
> * _This money could be used to address teacher shortages or provide health care for children for nearly a decade. We could use the money to functionally solve the budget crisis at rural hospitals. Instead, we risk wasting it on glitzy stadiums._
>

### Missourians know that the money can be better spent
Let's start with the baseline here: Kansas Citians were already asked to vote on a ballot measure that would increase their sales taxes to support stadium funding. And it was [emphatically defeated, by a 58%-42% margin](https://substack.com/redirect/bad19189-e9da-41a7-a0a1-72c0cb49650d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).3
I know respecting the will of voters [isn't always](https://substack.com/redirect/05b57cdb-b937-4086-a949-7d803ddd4b08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [the forte](https://substack.com/redirect/a40b1009-31a4-4446-95b8-ef9e380e7416?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the Missouri Legislature, but we should appreciate that they probably know what's best for their city.
Now, the [Governor is asking Missourians](https://substack.com/redirect/2ac0a084-db16-4df9-acaa-f749f377d7a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to pay **$1.5 billion in subsidies for new and improved stadiums**.4 That's a lot of money! For context, here are other options for how we could spend the money.5
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4ab2f91f-4550-43d4-b8b1-b18cb5cfb3cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Sources and additional information are in [Footnote #5](https://substack.com/redirect/cb5a0bc8-650e-4116-aa1e-bf284e1c8f1e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=164837845&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0ODM3ODQ1LCJpYXQiOjE3NDg4NzcxMDksImV4cCI6MTc1MTQ2OTEwOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.SyOgdH6T3N8wL-KVWeUqz3FykJqB8gj4EOJn0UivhrY)
We could solve the state's teacher shortage for eight years. We could make sure every child in the state has health insurance for 6.5 years. We could address nearly half of the state's crumbling road and bridge infrastructure. We could keep money-losing rural hospitals open for more than two decades!
Or we could piss the money away and dump it into two glitzy stadiums. With all of the state's issues, **it 's an outrage that this is a serious proposal.**
### The Governor's projected job data is complete nonsense
The Governor's Office claims that the Kansas City Chiefs [support over 4,500 jobs in Jackson County](https://substack.com/redirect/6fe09406-8a81-4096-9565-7f74d105529b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). At best, this is actively misleading; at worst, it's an outright lie.
Even if you include every player, coach, trainer, nutritionist, front-office employee, etc., you're looking at about 310 full-time employees.6
On game days, an NFL team might hire another [2,000 temp workers for about six hours of work](https://substack.com/redirect/2c5a2583-c9d0-48a6-9ca3-07cfb1424a88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But an NFL stadium gets used, at the _very_ most, 20 times per year.7
Yes, it's great to hire 2,000 people. But:
* Even if you generously assume that Arrowhead Stadium gets used 20 times per year, the staff there is only working 120 hours per year.
* That's about 115 [FTEs](https://substack.com/redirect/01a1c92b-d42f-4e75-a9d3-d8203a142e4b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which is not worth $100s of million in subsidies.8

So where on earth does 4,500 come from? If we want to be _really_ generous, maybe they're counting all of the short-term construction jobs and all of the very-part-time stadium jobs on game days.
But construction jobs only benefit workers who [would've otherwise been unemployed](https://substack.com/redirect/c484b786-ff07-4580-a86f-2f86160ee9b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Neither construction nor stadium funding [create any lasting, per-capita income growth](https://substack.com/redirect/2c5a2583-c9d0-48a6-9ca3-07cfb1424a88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
**In reality, it 's more like 425 sustained FTEs--less than 10% of what the Governor is claiming**. If we want to create jobs with taxpayer money, almost anything else would be more efficient.
### Promises of incremental tax revenue don't ever materialize
[Roger Noll](https://substack.com/redirect/bb667efe-99fb-4730-a3c3-2eecee4ff223?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a Stanford professor of economics who has written about public stadium funding, [says it most clearly](https://substack.com/redirect/a301ecf9-0d09-4289-9375-879a950088a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): "NFL stadiums do not generate significant local economic growth, and the incremental tax revenue is not sufficient to cover any significant financial contribution by the city."
The [St. Louis Fed](https://substack.com/redirect/a2ad94e6-1f4c-4f46-84dc-3dc4a0b16893?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has written about how little incremental tax revenue stadiums generate. This is especially damning:
> Of the 30 metro areas where the stadium or arena was built or refurbished in the previous 10 years, only three areas showed a significant relationship between the presence of a stadium and real per-capita personal income growth. And in all three cases--St. Louis, San Francisco/Oakland and Washington, D.C.--the relationship was _negative_.9
Even the [National Council of State Legislators](https://substack.com/redirect/e0a0d372-c664-4583-8d2b-0ad90d16ae9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), at the front lines of stadium subsidies, is unambiguous: "Much of the research indicates that the economic impact stadiums have on cities is negligible."
### Other reasons why this is a terrible use of taxpayer money
1. To the extent that stadiums create jobs, they really [just move them from one part of the metro area to another](https://substack.com/redirect/3073fde0-5de6-4813-b447-150545755ded?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's robbing Peter to pay Paul.
2. Taxpayer-funded stadiums [increase franchise value](https://substack.com/redirect/a2ad94e6-1f4c-4f46-84dc-3dc4a0b16893?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which allows owners--who are by definition fabulously wealthy--to sell their teams at [substantial profits](https://substack.com/redirect/a2ad94e6-1f4c-4f46-84dc-3dc4a0b16893?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The taxpayers pay, but the owners reap the (billions of dollars in) benefits.
3. Because of the way that stadiums are financed, cities--[including St. Louis](https://substack.com/redirect/bafe024a-8606-4caa-a6f5-97f0a518b14b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--often end up paying for stadiums [long after they close](https://substack.com/redirect/97a7e1c8-4c5a-45d6-aa8e-bf3b2f6a1812?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

### The Hunt family does not need our money
The Chiefs are owned by the [Hunt family](https://substack.com/redirect/a2b8f1a4-c32f-4ecb-8750-07f0f8483cf7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Their [family net worth is about $25 billion](https://substack.com/redirect/aa46e675-16c0-4a2e-b55f-18590ab5c1be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--they're the [12th-wealthiest](https://substack.com/redirect/3b74cb53-1269-4838-89e5-a56d32b40ac8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S.,10 and they're willing to offer up [less than half of their proposed](https://substack.com/redirect/ce5dfe31-c2ab-4ecd-9ab6-29d1412701ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Arrowhead Stadium renovations.
Not for nothing, it's not like the Hunts have ever acted like they really care that much about Missourians: they didn't want [the Rams moving to St. Louis](https://substack.com/redirect/3e49ad14-0276-48b5-8c9d-fb895816b961?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 1994, and they voted [for the Rams to leave Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/0e2ed8cc-12d9-413e-ba63-41d3ca468059?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2016.11
[Clark Hunt](https://substack.com/redirect/006aac55-dc10-4253-a0d8-954084a6a48e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and his half-brother [Lamar Hunt, Jr.](https://substack.com/redirect/5396b8b4-84fe-4374-b2e4-ce8cc959c303?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)12 both claim in their official bios to care about Kansas City. It's hard to take them seriously when, by threatening to leave, they're basically extorting the state to the tune of $100s of millions.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=164837845&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0ODM3ODQ1LCJpYXQiOjE3NDg4NzcxMDksImV4cCI6MTc1MTQ2OTEwOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.SyOgdH6T3N8wL-KVWeUqz3FykJqB8gj4EOJn0UivhrY)
### Where'd all the concern with efficiency go?
All of this is happening because the state of [Kansas is dangling tax breaks of its own](https://substack.com/redirect/51314d0a-86b0-43bb-8bd0-448beb9d1822?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to entice the Chiefs and the Royals across [State Line Road](https://substack.com/redirect/ad67bb79-f148-426d-bee5-2d040ac7688d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (It should be noted: these teams would still be in the Kansas City area even if they do move!13)
But there's already question of [whether Kansas can even afford](https://substack.com/redirect/5ba428c2-88d2-4cff-9aab-df8bf77c144d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the subsidies it's offering. I don't know why Missouri is so keen to engage them in a race to the bottom.
Missouri has [all sorts of problems with efficiency](https://substack.com/redirect/7eebd628-47f9-4a90-9e6e-4a2f86e2d678?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But this sort of rob-from-the-poor-and-give-to-the-rich policymaking would take things to a new level.
Look, [money alone doesn't fix our problems](https://substack.com/redirect/c0187f36-b47b-4ddd-9be3-f221659c61dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But with St. Louis still dealing with the [impact of tornado damage](https://substack.com/redirect/4e64471f-6134-4a59-8c8f-d662ede0e7a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and with the state comparing as poorly as it does to the rest of the country on [health](https://substack.com/redirect/f2fa5d1d-2d7b-4ae7-8809-59c851164f05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [crime](https://substack.com/redirect/98308530-70a0-4606-a501-d599ac2b1399?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [education](https://substack.com/redirect/0dc4880c-7f3f-4923-a67c-fad512ad869a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [road safety](https://substack.com/redirect/c18aa83f-7f9f-4e8c-8cfa-e01cdaffd5b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), how can we seriously consider spending [almost 10% of the state's general revenue](https://substack.com/redirect/e596af0d-46df-4bed-8169-4b421995327b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on something so clearly inefficient?
Our legislators have to vote this down.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTY0ODM3ODQ1LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ4ODc3MTA5LCJleHAiOjE3NTE0NjkxMDksImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.iRW_zF9vIc220r8mFbjujEPyjB6HUxUeRa9FQCPYWMg?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/102a8ac9-a54e-4de7-b791-653898666667?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
_Note: A few people have told me that these emails are sometimes going into spam folders. If you a)**mark the email as "not spam"** and b) **add bensamuels@substack.com to your contacts** , that should address the issue._
1
Maybe he should--I voted for him! ([In the primary](https://substack.com/redirect/d98e83bd-241b-4236-bdbd-e393a95a94e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), that is.)
2
The math here is (unfortunately) quite simple: "But the highest profile piece of Kehoe's special session agenda is _**his plan for the state to pay up to half the costs**_ of a new stadium for the Kansas City Royals and renovations to Arrowhead Stadium for the Kansas City Chiefs -- _**projects estimated to cost up to $3 billion**_." (Emphasis added; here's the [source](https://substack.com/redirect/2ac0a084-db16-4df9-acaa-f749f377d7a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
3
For context, Joe Biden was [closer to beating Donald Trump in Missouri in 2020](https://substack.com/redirect/59acf636-c670-4d3e-9d38-4896340534b4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than this ballot measure was to passing. And Joe Biden came nowhere close to winning Missouri.
4
To be specific: the funding would be for a [new stadium for the Royals and significant improvements to Arrowhead Stadium](https://substack.com/redirect/5cf277ce-364a-4e6b-9ed5-14b45f1070bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), home of the Chiefs.
5
I want to be explicit in providing my sourcing and math here, because it really is outrageous that we're thinking about doing this with the state's (limited) budget:
* _Missouri 's share of projected stadium costs_
* $1.5 billion, per [Footnote #2](https://substack.com/redirect/3da96927-3544-4d1c-95d0-a5d76d47a202?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), above.
* _Hire enough teachers to solve Missouri 's teacher shortage (1 year)_
* [As of 2023](https://substack.com/redirect/87fac395-ef58-436a-ad43-df86c13e483e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "Missouri has the equivalent of more than 3,500 full-time teacher vacancies."
* The [average salary for a teacher in Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/80315418-847b-45c8-b4a1-180e1220ea92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is $52,481.
* 3,500 × $52,481 = $184 million
* _Notes_ : This assumes that every teacher hired makes the average salary, which is probably not true--in all likelihood, they'd probably be making less, as teachers earlier in their careers. So in practice, $1.5 billion would go even further in offering a sustained solution to the state's teacher shortage.
* _Provide insurance for every uninsured child in Missouri (1 year)_
* There are [6,245,466 people in Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/f599134e-1800-4607-8a3b-9d1e620a8b68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* 22.2% are under 18, per the [same Census data](https://substack.com/redirect/f599134e-1800-4607-8a3b-9d1e620a8b68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* [5.4% of Missouri children](https://substack.com/redirect/11e341e8-b845-477b-b6ce-3e84e1f1f525?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) do not have health insurance.
* Nationally, [Medicaid spending per child](https://substack.com/redirect/4a825416-0c0f-47a3-9fb4-a48335459224?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is $3,023.
* 6,245,466 × 22.2% × 5.4% × $3,023 = $226 million
* _Notes_ : This is all oversimplified--a lot of this might ordinarily come from the federal government, for instance. Health care spending, and who is actually responsible for it, is complicated.
* _Address all of Missouri 's deferred road and bridge maintenance_
* There are [8,518,767 road miles](https://substack.com/redirect/288d37f5-6186-49da-a699-31d787f695ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the United States, of which 268,201 are in Missouri. So, 3.1% of U.S. road miles are in Missouri.
* "State and local governments face $105 billion in deferred maintenance for roads and bridges," [according to Pew](https://substack.com/redirect/4366d7a3-e9be-4ccf-8357-27cb0a45d615?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* $105 billion × 3.1% = $3.31 billion
* _Notes_ : This assumes that the need for road repairs are equally distributed by state and by road mile, which probably isn't true. But for our purposes, it's close enough.
* _Balance the budget at every money-losing rural hospital in Missouri (1 year)_
* There are [23 rural hospitals in Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/b4defdf7-35b9-4e36-8516-c90d012c31fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) running deficits.
* Data from the [Center for Healthcare Quality and Payment Reform](https://substack.com/redirect/e2646a70-f22a-4706-96d6-302ac843f433?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) outlines each hospital's total expenses and total margin.
* How much they're losing was calculated from their data. I added up the sum-total of deficits at those hospitals to get to $68.7 million.
* _Notes_ : The policy wouldn't play out _exactly_ like this--you'd want the support to be a bit broader than this, and it doesn't account for impending Medicaid cuts--but again, it gives us a rough sense.

There are a bunch of assumptions and simplifications in all of this; the exact figures would, of course, vary in the end. But it's clear that there's a _lot_ of social good that could be done if the government were willing to spend this money in a more reasonable way.
6
The math of this involves some guesswork, but let's use this:
* There are [53 players](https://substack.com/redirect/763d6694-e7bc-4a86-90da-ca2b1701239e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on an NFL roster.
* Teams have about [20-25 coaches](https://substack.com/redirect/0be554d4-6764-40ae-829c-09a47112cdaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), though this varies.
* According to _The Athletic_ , there are [175-220 people who travel](https://substack.com/redirect/9c065b30-aef0-43e4-8ce2-9e3e43e7fca9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with the team to each game, but some of them probably aren't employed by the team (e.g., spouses), since teams can [only travel with 110 staffers](https://substack.com/redirect/ccdf03c9-eec6-451d-bc50-b834a1e6fe6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), at least as of a couple of years ago. That includes coaches, so all in, that's another 85 people or so.
* The typical NFL team employs [125-175 full-time people](https://substack.com/redirect/2c5a2583-c9d0-48a6-9ca3-07cfb1424a88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in its front office. There's probably some overlap between this and the people who travel, but let's assume for the sake of being generous that there isn't.

53 players + roughly 25 coaches + roughly 85 staffers + roughly 150 front-office personnel is, very roughly, 313 people. I'm rounding to 310 just for ease of numbers.
7
Here's the math of how I get to 20, which is a _super_ generous assumption.
* An NFL team can play at home, at most, [14 times per year](https://substack.com/redirect/6d7e0ab9-c646-4894-99ea-c83b619cd012?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), assuming they don't host the Super Bowl. (That source didn't include preseason games.) For most teams, it's only their eight or nine annual home games, plus a possible preseason game or two.
* Let's say you include six stadium concerts on top of that, which is another super generous assumption. (For context, Arrowhead Stadium [only hosted three concerts](https://substack.com/redirect/8e0e5b3e-5117-4a53-8f9e-fa054765850f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2024.)

Because these assumptions are so generous, in reality, the job creation data is actually even worse--most NFL stadiums are only being used 13-15 times per year. I'm sure some of these stadiums get used for other things--high school football state championships, maybe--but having been to one of those games (and getting to [watch Ezekiel Elliott](https://substack.com/redirect/79384f11-c274-4b4d-ad4d-5f281a3f995a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) at what was then the [Edward Jones Dome](https://substack.com/redirect/d49811a5-1bf9-469b-9118-9437ecd4829b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), they're operating with a pretty bare-bones staff.
8
"Only" $100s of millions because another big chunk of the money is going to the Royals and their prospective new stadium development.
9
This is, in fairness, a study from 1994. But no one is suggesting that any of the underlying economics have changed.
10
The Hunt family appears to split its time between Missouri and Texas, but if you count them as Missourians, they're the wealthiest family in the state--wealthier than the [Busch](https://substack.com/redirect/7221a873-10a8-438a-a9cc-047fcf45ff1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or [Taylor](https://substack.com/redirect/f2588ab4-88a0-4f15-9564-85994403526d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) families.
11
It should be noted that Clark Hunt wasn't categorically in favor of relocation, and he actually [voted against the Chargers relocating from San Diego](https://substack.com/redirect/c712c147-ae1f-4fd1-8113-c217d2781ab4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). What it suggests is that he didn't want to share Missouri's media market, and he doesn't particularly care about what's best for Missourians.
12
At least as of 2015, Lamar Hunt, Jr. [owns a substantial portion of the Chiefs](https://substack.com/redirect/142ef00c-4eaf-4f13-9a51-2938d7e8e35c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
13
That's part of what makes all of this so ridiculous. They'd be moving, at most, 15 or 20 miles. This isn't going to have _any_ impact on the vast majority of Chiefs or Royals fans.

clean_body: Put simply: Missouri's plan is dumb. Put with more nuance: it's costly, inefficient, and a bad use of taxpayer money. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Put simply: Missouri's plan is dumb. Put with more nuance: it's costly, inefficient, and a bad use of taxpayer money. | | | Jun 2 | | | | | | | | | | | | On , I've mentioned in passing that government subsidies for professional sports stadiums are a terrible use of taxpayer money. But Missouri Governor Mike Kehoe doesn't listen to me,1 and today he called back Missouri lawmakers for a to provide in : a new stadium for the Royals, and renovations for the Chiefs. Missouri should reject this effort. It wastes taxpayer money, provides few tangible benefits, and keeps money from being spent more effectively--which is a low bar, because just about anything would be a more effective use of money. For those of you in Missouri:please forward this email to your friends and to your state legislators ; they're as we speak. who your state legislators are and how to reach them. use firstname.lastname@house.mo.gov and use firstname.lastname@senate.mo.gov for their official email addresses. ? Article summary: Kansas Citians already voted down public stadium funding. The evidence is overwhelming that it 's a bad use of taxpayer money. The jobs being created and supported by the Chiefs are, in reality, less than 10% of what the Missouri Governor 's Office is claiming. This money could be used to address teacher shortages or provide health care for children for nearly a decade. We could use the money to functionally solve the budget crisis at rural hospitals. Instead, we risk wasting it on glitzy stadiums. Missourians know that the money can be better spent Let's start with the baseline here: Kansas Citians were already asked to vote on a ballot measure that would increase their sales taxes to support stadium funding. And it was I know respecting the will of voters of the Missouri Legislature, but we should appreciate that they probably know what's best for their city. Now, the to pay $1.5 billion in subsidies for new and improved stadiums.4 That's a lot of money! For context, here are other options for how we could spend the money.5 | | ---|---|--- Sources and additional information are in . We could solve the state's teacher shortage for eight years. We could make sure every child in the state has health insurance for 6.5 years. We could address nearly half of the state's crumbling road and bridge infrastructure. We could keep money-losing rural hospitals open for more than two decades! Or we could piss the money away and dump it into two glitzy stadiums. With all of the state's issues, it 's an outrage that this is a serious proposal. The Governor's projected job data is complete nonsense The Governor's Office claims that the Kansas City Chiefs . At best, this is actively misleading; at worst, it's an outright lie. Even if you include every player, coach, trainer, nutritionist, front-office employee, etc., you're looking at about 310 full-time employees.6 On game days, an NFL team might hire another . But an NFL stadium gets used, at the very most, 20 times per year.7 Yes, it's great to hire 2,000 people. But: Even if you generously assume that Arrowhead Stadium gets used 20 times per year, the staff there is only working 120 hours per year. That's about 115 , which is not worth $100s of million in subsidies.8 So where on earth does 4,500 come from? If we want to be really generous, maybe they're counting all of the short-term construction jobs and all of the very-part-time stadium jobs on game days. But construction jobs only benefit workers who . Neither construction nor stadium funding . In reality, it 's more like 425 sustained FTEs--less than 10% of what the Governor is claiming. If we want to create jobs with taxpayer money, almost anything else would be more efficient. Promises of incremental tax revenue don't ever materialize , a Stanford professor of economics who has written about public stadium funding, : "NFL stadiums do not generate significant local economic growth, and the incremental tax revenue is not sufficient to cover any significant financial contribution by the city." The has written about how little incremental tax revenue stadiums generate. This is especially damning: Of the 30 metro areas where the stadium or arena was built or refurbished in the previous 10 years, only three areas showed a significant relationship between the presence of a stadium and real per-capita personal income growth. And in all three cases--St. Louis, San Francisco/Oakland and Washington, D.C.--the relationship was negative.9 Even the , at the front lines of stadium subsidies, is unambiguous: "Much of the research indicates that the economic impact stadiums have on cities is negligible." Other reasons why this is a terrible use of taxpayer money 1. To the extent that stadiums create jobs, they really . It's robbing Peter to pay Paul. 2. Taxpayer-funded stadiums , which allows owners--who are by definition fabulously wealthy--to sell their teams at . The taxpayers pay, but the owners reap the (billions of dollars in) benefits. 3. Because of the way that stadiums are financed, cities-- end up paying for stadiums . The Hunt family does not need our money The Chiefs are owned by the . Their the in the U.S.,10 and they're willing to offer up Arrowhead Stadium renovations. Not for nothing, it's not like the Hunts have ever acted like they really care that much about Missourians: they didn't want in 1994, and they voted in 2016.11 and his half-brother both claim in their official bios to care about Kansas City. It's hard to take them seriously when, by threatening to leave, they're basically extorting the state to the tune of $100s of millions. Where'd all the concern with efficiency go? All of this is happening because the state of to entice the Chiefs and the Royals across . (It should be noted: these teams would still be in the Kansas City area even if they do move!13) But there's already question of the subsidies it's offering. I don't know why Missouri is so keen to engage them in a race to the bottom. Missouri has . But this sort of rob-from-the-poor-and-give-to-the-rich policymaking would take things to a new level. Look, . But with St. Louis still dealing with the , and with the state comparing as poorly as it does to the rest of the country on and and and , how can we seriously consider spending on something so clearly inefficient? Our legislators have to vote this down. ? Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting. If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, . Note: A few people have told me that these emails are sometimes going into spam folders. If you a)mark the email as "not spam" and b) add bensamuels@substack.com to your contacts , that should address the issue. 1 Maybe he should--I voted for him! (, that is.) 2 The math here is (unfortunately) quite simple: "But the highest profile piece of Kehoe's special session agenda is his plan for the state to pay up to half the costs of a new stadium for the Kansas City Royals and renovations to Arrowhead Stadium for the Kansas City Chiefs -- projects estimated to cost up to $3 billion." (Emphasis added; here's the .) 3 For context, Joe Biden was than this ballot measure was to passing. And Joe Biden came nowhere close to winning Missouri. 4 To be specific: the funding would be for a , home of the Chiefs. 5 I want to be explicit in providing my sourcing and math here, because it really is outrageous that we're thinking about doing this with the state's (limited) budget: Missouri 's share of projected stadium costs $1.5 billion, per , above. Hire enough teachers to solve Missouri 's teacher shortage (1 year) , "Missouri has the equivalent of more than 3,500 full-time teacher vacancies." The is $52,481. 3,500 × $52,481 = $184 million Notes : This assumes that every teacher hired makes the average salary, which is probably not true--in all likelihood, they'd probably be making less, as teachers earlier in their careers. So in practice, $1.5 billion would go even further in offering a sustained solution to the state's teacher shortage. Provide insurance for every uninsured child in Missouri (1 year) There are . 22.2% are under 18, per the . do not have health insurance. Nationally, is $3,023. 6,245,466 × 22.2% × 5.4% × $3,023 = $226 million Notes : This is all oversimplified--a lot of this might ordinarily come from the federal government, for instance. Health care spending, and who is actually responsible for it, is complicated. Address all of Missouri 's deferred road and bridge maintenance There are in the United States, of which 268,201 are in Missouri. So, 3.1% of U.S. road miles are in Missouri. "State and local governments face $105 billion in deferred maintenance for roads and bridges," . $105 billion × 3.1% = $3.31 billion Notes : This assumes that the need for road repairs are equally distributed by state and by road mile, which probably isn't true. But for our purposes, it's close enough. Balance the budget at every money-losing rural hospital in Missouri (1 year) There are running deficits. Data from the outlines each hospital's total expenses and total margin. How much they're losing was calculated from their data. I added up the sum-total of deficits at those hospitals to get to $68.7 million. Notes : The policy wouldn't play out exactly like this--you'd want the support to be a bit broader than this, and it doesn't account for impending Medicaid cuts--but again, it gives us a rough sense. There are a bunch of assumptions and simplifications in all of this; the exact figures would, of course, vary in the end. But it's clear that there's a lot of social good that could be done if the government were willing to spend this money in a more reasonable way. 6 The math of this involves some guesswork, but let's use this: There are on an NFL roster. Teams have about , though this varies. According to The Athletic , there are with the team to each game, but some of them probably aren't employed by the team (e.g., spouses), since teams can , at least as of a couple of years ago. That includes coaches, so all in, that's another 85 people or so. The typical NFL team employs in its front office. There's probably some overlap between this and the people who travel, but let's assume for the sake of being generous that there isn't. 53 players + roughly 25 coaches + roughly 85 staffers + roughly 150 front-office personnel is, very roughly, 313 people. I'm rounding to 310 just for ease of numbers. 7 Here's the math of how I get to 20, which is a super generous assumption. An NFL team can play at home, at most, , assuming they don't host the Super Bowl. (That source didn't include preseason games.) For most teams, it's only their eight or nine annual home games, plus a possible preseason game or two. * Let's say you include six stadium concerts on top of that, which is another super generous assumption. (For context, Arrowhead Stadium in 2024.) Because these assumptions are so generous, in reality, the job creation data is actually even worse--most NFL stadiums are only being used 13-15 times per year. I'm sure some of these stadiums get used for other things--high school football state championships, maybe--but having been to one of those games (and getting to ) at what was then the , they're operating with a pretty bare-bones staff. 8 "Only" $100s of millions because another big chunk of the money is going to the Royals and their prospective new stadium development. 9 This is, in fairness, a study from 1994. But no one is suggesting that any of the underlying economics have changed. 10 The Hunt family appears to split its time between Missouri and Texas, but if you count them as Missourians, they're the wealthiest family in the state--wealthier than the or families. 11 It should be noted that Clark Hunt wasn't categorically in favor of relocation, and he actually . What it suggests is that he didn't want to share Missouri's media market, and he doesn't particularly care about what's best for Missourians. 12 At least as of 2015, Lamar Hunt, Jr. . 13 That's part of what makes all of this so ridiculous. They'd be moving, at most, 15 or 20 miles. This isn't going to have any impact on the vast majority of Chiefs or Royals fans. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Ben Samuels 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: Steve Scalise
subject: I need to bring this to your attention:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
This is a serious problem – and it couldn’t have come at a worse time.

| |
---
| | | **OFFICIAL REPUBLICAN COMMUNICATION**
---
Hey there...

This is Steve Scalise. I need to bring this to your attention. It's urgent.

_I 'm sorry to report that we just FAILED to hit our May fundraising goal. That's the first major fundraising miss ALL YEAR..._

_You can bet that Democrats are going to be over the moon and taking a victory lap at the expense of our most vulnerable Conservatives._ This is a serious problem - and it couldn't have come at a worse time.

Especially because the latest FEC reports show the Democrats beat us by 1,000,000+ dollars last month. They now have the resources to unload on their ENTIRE list of 35 GOP-held House targets.

**Desperate times call for desperate measures. That 's why I'm extending our internal fundraising deadline for another 24 hours.** I RARELY do this but I know we can close the gap in our budgets if this message reaches the right people.

**[And I'm really hoping you're one of them.](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zmuefa08jq7fe7g0siv1ep7ikm9q8taiti8bti2g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c5h2bs "And I’m really hoping you’re one of them.")**

My team and I crunched the numbers and we fell short by about 12% of our goal. That's a big miss.

We can either call it a day and slash our voter contact budgets and early messaging programs...

_... OR we can dig deep and close that gap ASAP! _

I know that these key campaign initiatives make the difference between victory and defeat in tight races so I'm choosing the latter.

Is there ANYTHING I can say to convince you to pitch in today?

**[Even five or ten bucks would help. More than that would make an even bigger impact. Any amount you can spare would make a huge difference.](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zqmmu6nmrb2vfcr8dg7mgp2fbejduc3g14hdf670_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c5h2bs "Even five or ten bucks would help. More than that would make an even bigger impact. Any amount you can spare would make a huge difference.")** And if you can't afford anything right now, that's okay - I know how much you've done for us so far. I'll never forget that.

**SO PLEASE:**
---
| | | [ADD FIFTY BUCKS HERE](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2z0t0apfes6kf2o0g9v28cqei2vkes12ausg811go_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c5h2bs)
---
| | | [ADD TWENTY BUCKS HERE](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zuerrftmu80ejffa846ji1149mqnd24fndt3ihjg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c5h2bs)
---
| | | [ADD TEN BUCKS HERE](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zelv87bsidili3fs1f7d3g1fq8nl9er8u0d1sfc0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c5h2bs)
---

Thanks for all your hard work,

- Steve Scalise

---
**OFFICIAL REPUBLICAN COMMUNICATION**
---

---
| |
* * *
---
\- Democrat Leadership formed an _anti-Trump task force_
\- More than a dozen Attorneys General are _suing_ the Trump Administration
\- A House Democrat just introduced _7 Articles of Impeachment_

---
| [](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zrocg5l0j9u9pti82c3a9502ch9mft39og2o8feo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c5h2bs "Liberal Trump Resistance")
---

If they manage to strip us of our House Majority, they'll have the power to impeach, investigate, and bring his entire legislative agenda to a SCREECHING HALT!

**We're PLEADING WITH YOU to do your part to help SAVE our Pro-Trump House Majority:**
---
| | | [DO YOUR PART: PITCH IN NOW](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2z82ir9hfnbeco1eotkkmlsa54a0knn27q2adq9l0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c5h2bs "DO YOUR PART: PITCH IN NOW")
---
| | Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC.
---

Team Scalise is committed to restoring a Conservative House majority and electing Conservatives across the country. Thank you for being a part of our movement. This message was sent because you are a member of the Team Scalise Conservative grassroots network.

subject: PBS GONE ?!

body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5) [**WE CAN’T BELIEVE THIS FROM _NPR_ → “Trump plans order to cut funding for _NPR_ and _PBS_ ”](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61656?t=3&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5)** [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61656?t=4&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5)
| [SIGN THE EMERGENCY PETITION TO STAND WITH _PBS_ →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61656?t=5&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5)
---
* * *
Donald Trump and Marjorie Taylor Greene are fighting to defund programs like Sesame Street, _PBS_ , and _NPR_. They’re claiming that Sesame Street brainwashes viewers and children with “communist” agendas and promotes “anti-American” views.

We refuse to let _PBS_ and Sesame Street get cancelled by the GOP’s extremist agenda.

[ **_We need you and every other person reading this email to sign our Emergency Petition. Help us gather 30,000 signatures before 9:00 AM EST tomorrow >>**_](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61656?t=6&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5)
[SIGN NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61656?t=7&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5)
---
It’s critically important we fight back. If we fail:
**→ FIRST: Millions of Americans would lose access to educational and news programming.

→ SECOND: Beloved programs like Sesame Street, Curious George, and local sports coverage would be SHUT DOWN.

→ THIRD: The GOP’s hateful, anti-journalist agenda will gain momentum. Their lies about ‘propaganda’ inside public programming will continue dividing our country.**
A top executive at _PBS_ put it perfectly: “There’s nothing more American than PBS.”

We know that many Americans agree with us that we can’t let the GOP’s political games result in us losing educational staples like Sesame Street. But agreeing isn’t enough, we need you to sign our emergency petition.
[ **_SIGN NOW: Add your name to our emergency petition calling on Congress to protect _PBS_ and programs like Sesame Street →_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61656?t=8&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5)
[SIGN NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61656?t=9&akid=23099%2E4449941%2E2MPes5)
---
Thanks,

_Fight for Reform_
_PBS_ is not associated or affiliated with Fight for Reform and has not sponsored or approved of this communication.

name: House GOP Rapid Response
subject: INCOMING DISTRESS SIGNAL
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
We’re scrambling

|
---
| | | This should concern every America First Patriot.

**[Our House GOP grassroots fundraising is in trouble. Take a look:](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z4o77nd9945j54n0a8li3rtafo9b316ln5rvdr98_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c9h2bs "Our House GOP grassroots fundraising is in trouble. Take a look:")**

---
| [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zlr49g6i3qrpnt4a5mv650sebsdq64hupf0v5ujo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c9h2bs)
---

**The Numbers Don't Lie:** Our average gift has TANKED compared to this time last year. Donations are drying up, volunteer hours are dwindling, and engagement is at an all-time low. It's like the air has been sucked out of our sails.

**The Question Is:** **[Are we going to let the ship sink, or are we going to come together to save it?](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z23qke7sfnoltcqsbgf3bmc712viuicrvh25ro9g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c9h2bs "Are we going to let the ship sink, or are we going to come together to save it?")** We're not just talking about our majority in Congress - we're talking about the future of our country.

---
| | | [SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zkugub27k9b2agq2cjobh4i23gq291j245e7ucjo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c9h2bs "SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING")
---

**This is Critical:** **[We need 1,000 conservatives like you to respond to this distress signal.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zmvsm0oqlh5u7mv45fg2iagddvb01ji4pd9mt1k0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c9h2bs "We need 1,000 conservatives like you to respond to this distress signal.")** We need you to chip in, spread the word, and help us rebuild our support. The fate of our movement is in your hands.

**Time is Running Out:** We can't keep the ship afloat forever.**[If we don't act now, it's going to be too late. Will you answer the call and help us save our movement?](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zspdmk739vcmn9lsthj63vflhs5g4947d4pnn3uo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c9h2bs "If we don't act now, it's going to be too late. Will you answer the call and help us save our movement?")**

---
| | | [SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z85kgel4tb8nh1raf4bnn2qmqebj5lgaul9ve0a8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3ie4or2c9h2bs "SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING")

name: Jasmine Crockett
subject: Dictatorship? DENIED.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio House Democratic Campaign Committee, 545 E Town St
body:
|
---
Y’all, thank god for the courts. **They just stopped Trump from dismantling the Department of Education.** Newsflash, MAGA: you don’t get to gut public education without Congress. Thousands of workers? _**Saved.**_
Public education? _**Protected.**_
Dictatorship? _**Denied.**_ **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108838104/549169318/273633830?refcode=250602_Jasmine_Denied&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYjRiODVlMS1jNzNmLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X_Tw9TEM8pxYP0-FcsvmtfV5p0D5V6tGf30KBPjEei0=)** I’ll be blunt: **At this point, the Republicans are the party of feelings over facts.** Whatever they feel, is what they go with — regardless of what the law says or whether or not there’s any supporting evidence. When it comes to the federal government, it doesn’t work like that. **We have a judicial system and legal processes for a reason, and you don’t just get to ignore it because of how you “feel.”** But the Republican majorities in Congress will never stand up to Trump like that — so it’s time we elect people who will. That’s what I’m fighting to do each and every day from now until Election Day — and I need your help like never before. [**If you’re committed to replacing the MAGA House Majority with strong House Democrats, join me in the fight by chipping in $10, $25 or more, split with Ohio House Dems. We can’t do this without you and we can’t afford anyone on the sidelines. >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108838105/549169319/273633830?refcode=250602_Jasmine_Denied&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYjRiODVlMS1jNzNmLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X_Tw9TEM8pxYP0-FcsvmtfV5p0D5V6tGf30KBPjEei0=) | | [**$10 »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108838106/549169320/273633830?amount=10&refcode=250602_Jasmine_Denied&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYjRiODVlMS1jNzNmLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X_Tw9TEM8pxYP0-FcsvmtfV5p0D5V6tGf30KBPjEei0= "https://secure.actblue.com/")

clean_body: | Y’all, thank god for the courts. They just stopped Trump from dismantling the Department of Education. Newsflash, MAGA: you don’t get to gut public education without Congress. Thousands of workers? Saved. Public education? Protected. Dictatorship? Denied. = I’ll be blunt: At this point, the Republicans are the party of feelings over facts. Whatever they feel, is what they go with — regardless of what the law says or whether or not there’s any supporting evidence. When it comes to the federal government, it doesn’t work like that. We have a judicial system and legal processes for a reason, and you don’t just get to ignore it because of how you “feel.” But the Republican majorities in Congress will never stand up to Trump like that — so it’s time we elect people who will. That’s what I’m fighting to do each and every day from now until Election Day — and I need your help like never before. = | | = "" | = "" | = "" | = "" Thank you, Jasmine = = = OHDC 545 E. body:
| | | Hi there team, I'm Devan, and I'm the Executive Director of the DSCC.

Over the next two years, it'll be my job to develop our winning strategy to put Democrats in the _strongest_ position possible to flip the Senate blue. But I can't do that without insights from supporters like you, so one of my top priorities as Executive Director is to build a team of Democrats to inform my advisors.

**They told me that you were in our top ten percent of supporters last year -- so it's clear that I can rely on _you._ That's why I'm asking you to take my new strategy survey:**

[**Please, click here to enter my official Democratic strategy survey >>**](https://go.dscc.org/go/21654?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&akid=20396%2E9716520%2EtEY19T)

| | [ENTER SURVEY](https://go.dscc.org/go/21654?ask=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&akid=20396%2E9716520%2EtEY19T)
---

With competitive elections all over the map and _even more_ MAGA loyalists up for re-election, we have a golden opportunity here. **We have the chance to unseat far-right Republicans AND replace Trump's worst enablers with strong Democrats who will fight for our rights.**

But in order to lock in our victory, I need to gather critical data immediately from motivated supporters like you. **I'm _100% confident_ your insights will sharpen our strategy.**

[**So please, will you accept the invitation to join my strategy survey before the midnight deadline? I'm waiting to review your responses before I make my next move, so please click here to enter the survey now >>**](https://go.dscc.org/go/21654?ask=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&akid=20396%2E9716520%2EtEY19T)

| [ENTER SURVEY](https://go.dscc.org/go/21654?ask=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250602-Devan-NewEDStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&akid=20396%2E9716520%2EtEY19T)
---

I'm looking forward to hearing from you. **Together, we'll lead the fight to win back our Senate majority.**

Thank you,

Devan
Executive Director, DSCC

name: Andy Kim
subject: A time to recommit ourselves to the fight for equality for everyone
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey
body:
Let’s come together – not just this month, but every month – to build a country that lives up to its promise of liberty and justice for all.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108838714/549171250/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTQ1MjE0Ny1jOTNmLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oMSA9qKTm5GrzjLHAKfk9u-geDAoM-0QAJBvY94CRfw=&emci=47a73f47-c33f-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=e5452147-c93f-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=26261389)
---

| | Pride Month is a time to celebrate love, dignity, and the unwavering courage of the LGBTQ+ community. It's also a time to recommit ourselves to the fight for equality – _for everyone._ All Americans, no matter their race, faith, sexuality, gender, or political affiliation, deserve equal rights and protections under the law. **That's why I'm a proud cosponsor of the Equality Act – a bill that would guarantee civil rights protections for LGBTQ+ Americans.** This legislation would:
* Amend existing civil rights laws to explicitly include sexual orientation and gender identity
* Prohibit discrimination in public spaces, services, and federally funded programs
* Ensure that no one is denied the basic freedoms we promise to every American

**Right now, LGBTQ+ kids and adults face discrimination, harassment, and fear just for being who they are. That's unacceptable. We must do better.** [**If you believe, like I do, that everyone deserves to live with dignity and be treated equally under the law, I'm asking you to take a moment to show your support. clean_body: Let’s come together – not just this month, but every month – to build a country that lives up to its promise of liberty and justice for all. | | | | Pride Month is a time to celebrate love, dignity, and the unwavering courage of the LGBTQ+ community. It's also a time to recommit ourselves to the fight for equality – for everyone. All Americans, no matter their race, faith, sexuality, gender, or political affiliation, deserve equal rights and protections under the law. That's why I'm a proud cosponsor of the Equality Act – a bill that would guarantee civil rights protections for LGBTQ+ Americans. This legislation would: Amend existing civil rights laws to explicitly include sexual orientation and gender identity Prohibit discrimination in public spaces, services, and federally funded programs Ensure that no one is denied the basic freedoms we promise to every American Right now, LGBTQ+ kids and adults face discrimination, harassment, and fear just for being who they are. That's unacceptable. We must do better. Let's come together – not just this month, but every month – to build a country that lives up to its promise of liberty and justice for all. name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: re: AIPAC and Crypto
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

It’s time to raise the alarm,
Peter: AIPAC, Crypto, and their affiliated dark
money super PACs are coming for the Squad and other Justice Democrats in
2026.

The American Israel Public Affairs Committee (AIPAC) takes checks from
Trump-supporting millionaire and billionaire megadonors and lobbies
Congress for unconditional support for Israel while spending hundreds of
millions of dollars attacking progressive, pro-Palestinian candidates in
Democratic primaries.

They did it last cycle when they unseated progressive champions Cori Bush
and Jamaal Bowman, and reports indicate they’re getting ready to do it all
over again in 2026, setting their sights on remaining Squad members like
Rep. Ilhan Omar.

In a new Politico article, the spokesperson for one of AIPAC’s super PACs
said they’re “looking at both Republican and Democratic opportunities” to
expand “the pro-Israel bipartisan majority in Congress” and “anticipates
being highly-involved in a number of races opposing anti-Israel
incumbents, defending pro-Israel Members of Congress against anti-Israel
challengers, and in open seats where there is a detractor of the US-Israel
relationship.” To summarize, they’re gearing up to take down any Democrat
with the courage to support a permanent ceasefire in Gaza and oppose the
genocide of Palestinians.

Similarly, the pro-Crypto group Fairshake PAC, which has spent millions
against Justice Democrats candidates and progressives in previous
elections, said “everything is on the table.”

AIPAC and Crypto were two of the largest political spenders in the 2024
cycle, and they’re clearly getting ready to spend even more in 2026 to try
to destroy our progressive movement of leaders who put people before
profits.

They want to unseat the Squad completely to maintain the status quo and
their grip on power in Washington. This is a David vs. Goliath fight
between peace and genocide, between the working class and billionaire
elites. We need to prepare NOW.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250602?refcode=em20250602-36728&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36728_1203867_M0zc58&akid=36728%2E1203867%2EM0zc58 ]The only way we’re able to go up against the war chests of powerful
opponents like AIPAC and the Crypto lobby is by building a foundation of
grassroots resources from everyday people like you. Pitch in
$5 or more right now to ensure we’re ready for the fight →

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250602?refcode=em20250602-36728&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36728_1203867_M0zc58&akid=36728%2E1203867%2EM0zc58 ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250602-36728-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36728_1203867_M0zc58&akid=36728%2E1203867%2EM0zc58

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36728%2E1203867%2EM0zc58

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: House GOP Rapid Response
subject: INCOMING DISTRESS SIGNAL
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
We’re scrambling

|
---
| | | This should concern every America First Patriot.

**[Our House GOP grassroots fundraising is in trouble. Take a look:](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zebnh340ggapsec08sd3jmeqebmr71lpfmp8smvg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3iecpb1cph2bs "Our House GOP grassroots fundraising is in trouble. Take a look:")**

---
| [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z2knl1n6shns3maqdqn16efshtdsapmrmh5hn2d8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3iecpb1cph2bs)
---

**The Numbers Don't Lie:** Our average gift has TANKED compared to this time last year. Donations are drying up, volunteer hours are dwindling, and engagement is at an all-time low. It's like the air has been sucked out of our sails.

**The Question Is:** **[Are we going to let the ship sink, or are we going to come together to save it?](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z7s8b0jjth41jlh2g05uv5d8gulu01999ot8t3t8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3iecpb1cph2bs "Are we going to let the ship sink, or are we going to come together to save it?")** We're not just talking about our majority in Congress - we're talking about the future of our country.

---
| | | [SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zigtn2fsqvsd67rf3mcld8pmc258daote6ttooq8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3iecpb1cph2bs "SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING")
---

**This is Critical:** **[We need 1,000 conservatives like you to respond to this distress signal.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z393jmn52jo6h8f3j09lidgsm2ulg6vmerr9ag7g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3iecpb1cph2bs "We need 1,000 conservatives like you to respond to this distress signal.")** We need you to chip in, spread the word, and help us rebuild our support. The fate of our movement is in your hands.

**Time is Running Out:** We can't keep the ship afloat forever.**[If we don't act now, it's going to be too late. Will you answer the call and help us save our movement?](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zkl3jhmve5nmbjpih1pv48krpoe1921t4hcrdm7o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3iecpb1cph2bs "If we don't act now, it's going to be too late. Will you answer the call and help us save our movement?")**

---
| | | [SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zmt1emm58gu6j4vtbhv603rnohhi3tck61hr1v2o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0s3iecpb1cph2bs "SAVE OUR FUNDRAISING")

name: AG Jason Miyares
subject: Politics shouldn’t come before public safety
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
Friend, they’re focused on everything but you.| | | Friend, it’s happening again.

My Democrat opponents just hit the airwaves, with attack ads slamming me and all we’ve accomplished to keep Virginians safe — funded by **MILLIONS** from their far-Left mega-donors looking to flip the Commonwealth blue.

But, if you listen closely to what they’re saying... You’ll notice something: _**They’re focused on everything except YOU.**_

**Radical Jay Jones** dragged out disgraced former Gov. Ralph Northam to brag about _**SUING** Governor Glenn Youngkin — _with your tax dollars.__
---
[](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/Q5I5x5Zc2TzSmDaiH8N-X-6UzutPZKQ4ZnR6o6TbTh89DXZ00wf-sSi3BoZZW5nIujGl7FKOg0AS6Hv-GTGgnhiUE2geb_rWvlcQNQ50mqi-qZzYecGbI7dAytxXDyBB4S2hjtSKMI2D8XiQoLtZy61BmMsfJtctW9b14-iq-TSEI2g8CWD9yLxs1iwHo5mRzR2aCT9Rxc-v_dHq6bNt82Q4bUVBOZ9kDKazaUjTExLbFyIG4BxJW8yLrp5-a-N-Ys8kApYCODq7pS35UJXyo65UCzTRO1josmUPNoOnc68LdcZfBd7Dc7N5YGrio0BFL86HbkD_uON9UNkiQA9pU11Xf-xCYXyn-OmlX-lIn7BhONWMM_OmCAR5k6g/dYTREz_1JUG7eeJQZRANpjKJ-PE8-1gO/9)
---
**“Progressive Prosecutor” Shannon Taylor** promised her top priority as AG would be to _**“get back at”** Elon Musk and President Trump... _Yes, you read that right.__
---
[ **SUPPORT THE PEOPLE'S PROTECTOR** ](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/UCkVXTtiuaAxqK73xNtsWmgVxc1h9cXTan2nWCKYWz5OSndLo3gRESd5I45WtnUF1z9ceJc1kT4HyYao-g5BKh4rY9kqGH9BBHpfRf9aGlvjjNbRy8Z1c8Ecn6sDyLXjCU52OqBUekc2KzsxHSiClftKpw69gXB4lt_IgJVPXceUCK_XtFeaUIry98-zPVbcvcOfX-Xigst6NrOamjVY0MdaLRYhQlexC5TERSsvOS1P98sWWomyg4TkmBanPXKvbeMEmXCmGRGJSkRexif1pBSaAKlvtAKwWxgJmMHa6Qa6C7CAp0WrXzlxEU65K3Sa6PfIdvSYO9gKrLn_DO2pQjrPTWwHsJaU0Jz0YShkV7VNRSPTIdKm4HAfAB8/0o3s3PlBMWJHeylosf7Mpdnj1AOp40vC/9)
---
This election isn’t about _“getting back”_ at anyone or settling political scores with public figures—[ _**it's about fighting for YOU, period.**_](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/to23seUtrXKd0JQdWPbx6Gr7v2B3xQWFGI02gQ7GdQLbwpPfxJnmJVl_S1SPeIg4VVlRG6xyT-3tVrwyXPgNx-vgnk3dQNg03CXzNt8T8ocAJLIJUqhns2RHGjjWdF0sp2QtxBojXz-6uV_s9CcUvgocSOMBSdcEKeCnQFosMq2gwy0JQEZQ0sc6muBSfqdRmbsYdlM05amQWQID9gB5HjmyH6FnNinaesTQaeh-_2m4xcaVCccmMzuCoziWFUsNPJDdD87h_lushflmcpo9A7ToxcC6TSRkD0TgWRrPVYJ6zv5Gcdu4hnZBpWni8FjX-LMla0kGk2KK06Usj9DZ-GPHXm28-ARMyc0USl9_KC6FZZJTe-YjL_1_v5A/FWNW5Wvv77JAwKEGDxBt5Y8ktNHMEYCK/9)

While they spend money running away from their records, **my record continues to speak for itself.** As The People’s Protector, we have:
---
_➡️ _Slashed murders by 30% statewide_
➡️ _Won over $1 billion from Big Pharma to fight addiction_
➡️ _Protected over 300,000 children with Child ID kits_
➡️ _Reduced fentanyl deaths by 44% — one of the largest drops in the nation__
---
And we’re just getting started, friend. Virginians deserve an Attorney General who will prosecute violent crime, cut the murder rate, protect children, and stop illicit drugs. **NOT someone using the office to wage partisan political battles.**

_**[If you believe, like me, that Virginia’s top cop should focus on YOU and not play political games, chip in to our campaign today.](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/tRZC5_Bb6NpBt97O2WuFJ5LjJUBDNLaTC5ZiHlResO3MeRgr_QE6FRwW7XZVra_Rxf6sFQQIujfvpAd93ybJoh4oQO48lDNojDUD_Aw1dsLUCon1Vhg2rHqWSui5gghE6k-TvstI44M1bliyC-n3IaC_YBaAaAWKdr5ORRAfbIlE9t3x2mDbbEFT_oBSJWW7jyyBoDeZnlEfZ0qm3IUFTna1nxoS4F5rr6CPJkQUufvd49vfdooZMdhcssoLwF3NYHCrkH6eZfQTTGXEgjCp2mtklW4GT0Uhun8-LjIeIVO5MkTIk2xpAfjWVm_3UZrHCYArYicjPUzepJwaMCxaoSmixUU4AKf4VAqd6fQ6ROPLva7NjOnoAo3UGEw/HCg0ORHN2y64AJ0Ln7OsOP-MHvkaox3Z/9)**_
---

---
_Let's keep fighting for Virginia,__**Attorney General Jason Miyares**_

# YOU'RE INVITED TO ATTEND THE MOMS FOR LIBERTY "WINNING WORKSHOP" AIMED AT HELPING YOU SUCCESSFULLY RUN FOR YOUR SCHOOL BOARD!
---
|
---
| **MOMS FOR LIBERTY "WINNING WORKSHOP" • CAMPAIGN TRAINING EVENT
JUNE 21, 2024 at 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM
Andrews House • 39 West Winter Street • Delaware, OH 43015**
You can register for the winning workshop here: [**REGISTRATION INVITATION LINK**](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=7bb8500c6d&e=5c740fa3e2)

**Pricing:**
* Non-Members Registration $30.00
* **Member Registration $0.00 (not a member, consider become a member - its free!!! Join the Fight | Moms for Liberty )**

**Why Attend?**

**Grassroots leaders & members** – walk away knowing how you can more **effectively help your preferred candidates RUN and WIN** for your school boards with resources like our Campaign Kit!

**Candidates or Potential Candidates** – you’ll work through our **Campaign Kit** – our premier Primer & **Done-for-You** tool, **custom-created** for first-time, less-experienced, or previously unsuccessful school board candidates to successfully **RUN and WIN!**

If you have any **questions** , feel free to contact **Sarah Simon** at [**sarah@momsforliberty.com**](mailto:sarah@momsforliberty.com). Also, if you know a possible school board candidate or grassroot leader that could benefit from this training, please forward the invite.
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| |
# MARCELL STRBICH
FOR OHIO SECRETARY OF STATE
FIRST CAMPAIGN FILING DEADLINE:
**JUNE 30TH!**
---
|
**MARCELL STRBICH IS RUNNING FOR
OHIO SECRETARY OF STATE
AS A REPUBLICAN POLITICAL OUTSIDER!**
[**strbichforohio.com**](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=ca64b8f70e&e=5c740fa3e2)
Many across Ohio already know: Marcell is the right person for this job. He’s a combat veteran, devoted family man, God-fearing patriot, and a proven election integrity advocate. Marcell has been instrumental in drafting nearly every meaningful election integrity bill proposed in Ohio—legislation that’s been deliberately ignored by two consecutive General Assemblies.

Until we see real campaign finance reform, grassroots Ohioans are going to need to dig deep, open their wallets, and be willing to make a personal financial commitment.

_**"In politics, money talks, and campaign filings are how that message gets delivered—loud and clear."**_

It’s important for political candidates to show strong fundraising success in their required government filings because, WHETHER WE LIKE IT OR NOT, money signals momentum, viability, and power—both to insiders and voters. Here’s why it matters:
* Big fundraising numbers make a candidate look like a serious contender. Media, party leaders, donors, and voters all use these filings to gauge who’s competitive and worth taking seriously. Strong fundraising hauls get picked up in headlines: “Candidate Raises $250K in First Quarter”. This creates free publicity and positions the campaign as rising or dominant.
* Party insiders, elected officials, and political organizations often withhold endorsements until they see who can raise money and win. High fundraising can trigger snowball support from power brokers who want to back a winner. Strong numbers can scare off challengers, who may not want to go up against a financially strong opponent.
* Grassroots supporters want to feel they’re backing a campaign that has momentum and a path to victory. Public fundraising reports serve as a psychological boost to volunteers and potential new donors.
* Fundraising equals field operations, advertising, legal support, and more. A candidate who’s raising well is seen as having the infrastructure to compete.

Fundraising filings are more than compliance—they’re performance scorecards. They influence media, endorsements, donor psychology, and even the ballot itself.

**Marcell's first deadline is June 30th.** Shortly after that, his filings are due and the public, including potential donors & the media, will gauge Marcell's financial competitiveness.

**BECAUSE OHIO ONLY REQUIRES A JUNE 30TH AND DECEMBER 31ST FILINGS THIS YEAR, MARCELL WILL NOT HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY TO SHOW FUNDRAISING MUSCLE TO THE PUBLIC UNTIL 2026!**

**THIS IS WHY IT'S IMPORTANT THAT IF YOU'RE GOING TO DONATE YOU DO IT NOW, AND DONATE AS MUCH AS YOU CAN!**

Here's how to donate:

**DONATE ONLINE:**
[**https://secure.anedot.com/strbichforohio/donate**](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=74cb0053fc&e=5c740fa3e2)

**DONATE BY CHECK:**
**Strbich for Ohio
PO Box 21174
Roanoke, VA 24018**

Please text 614-499-0599 if you need ACH or wire transfer information.

_**Maximum donation is $16,615.67.**_

**THANK YOU! **
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | **GFI NEWSLETTER ARCHIVE**
---
|

---
|
---

Best regards,

**Mark Pukita
A GFI Person
mark@gfiohio.com
614-499-0599 (c)
[GFIOhio.com](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=ecba24b54b&e=5c740fa3e2)**

**Our mailing address is:**
The Grassroots Freedom Initiative4240 Hobbs Landing Dr WDublin, OH 43017-6025
[Add us to your address book](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/vcard?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=9877b1cc31)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/profile?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=9877b1cc31&e=5c740fa3e2&c=f5800c6dfa) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=9877b1cc31&t=b&e=5c740fa3e2&c=f5800c6dfa).

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3d09295a-d9b8-4a85-a288-ae3a733cd694?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

> Read the most recent FREE subscriber email [here](https://substack.com/redirect/b4868fa9-c6e8-40ae-ab66-c2b10aa84c28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/e0172584-3bb4-4d9b-b452-96c37f0bcb7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas attorney general won 't get new powers to prosecute election crimes after legislative deadlock,**" Votebeat / Texas Tribune's **Natalie Contreras** - "Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** appears unlikely to gain new powers to prosecute election crimes after the House and Senate failed Saturday to reach a deadline to work out their differences on how much leeway to grant the state's top legal office in such cases.

That development in the waning days of the regular 2025 legislative session ending Monday comes two years after the House voted to impeach Paxton on misconduct allegations that included bribery and abuse of office. The Senate later acquitted Paxton.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/09b424b3-64bf-42d9-9d99-a12c37d2922d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[House Bill 5138](https://substack.com/redirect/bd856d4c-7882-4932-99ac-5d06ebeea25f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was filed by state Rep. **Matt Shaheen** , a Republican from North Texas. The Senate passed a version that would have given the attorney general's office the power to unilaterally prosecute local election crimes when a county fails to do so. The House had previously agreed to grant Paxton's office more powers to prosecute such cases, but only after local prosecutors considered the case.

Shaheen filed the bill after the Texas Court of Criminal Appeals, the state's highest criminal court, found Paxton's office does not have authority to independently prosecute criminal election cases in trial courts without the request of a local prosecutor.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/09b424b3-64bf-42d9-9d99-a12c37d2922d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Paxton [later targeted three of the Republican judges](https://substack.com/redirect/45519536-0a4f-4391-bf0d-3b52723179f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who reached that decision when they were up for reelection, leading a successful campaign to [oust them during the 2024 GOP primary](https://substack.com/redirect/c91fc233-b487-40ac-b1ad-2bd4d4c79254?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The legislation would have explicitly granted Paxton the authority he sought in the court case.

[Paxton is running for U.S. Senate](https://substack.com/redirect/24bc26ff-07d1-4167-ac45-d51eaf5234a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against Republican incumbent**John Cornyn** , creating an opening for a new state attorney general to take office in 2027.

Under the Senate's version, local law enforcement would be required to send a report to the attorney general's office if it believes there is probable cause to suspect that an election crime has been committed. The attorney general's office would then have the authority to prosecute. Local prosecutors and law enforcement would be required to turn over any information they have about election crime investigations to the attorney general's office.

Under the House's version of the bill, law enforcement agencies would be required to notify the attorney general's office when reporting a potential election crime to local prosecutors. The attorney general could investigate only if the local prosecutor took no action on those reports for six months. And under the bill, local prosecutors could challenge the attorney general's assertion of jurisdiction to investigate.

A conference committee failed to reach an agreement by midnight Saturday, effectively killing the bill. Texas Gov. **Greg Abbott** could call a special session on the issue if he chooses.

[Paxton](https://substack.com/redirect/ce2104f5-6a76-4e7d-ad4b-60f210e817d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has made targeting election-related crimes a priority since he took office in 2015. He has pursued [more than 300 investigations](https://substack.com/redirect/3678747a-4c0b-4c68-92da-813baea12dfe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of suspected crimes by voters but has successfully convicted only a handful.

Republican lawmakers filed [similar legislation in 2023](https://substack.com/redirect/5ce82235-b884-461a-baad-1d44bbbd4696?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to give him the authority to step into local investigations, but those bills failed to pass. That was the same regular session in which the House impeached Paxton.

Some voting rights advocates have said giving the Texas attorney general more power over local law enforcement offices is [unconstitutional. They warned](https://substack.com/redirect/5ce82235-b884-461a-baad-1d44bbbd4696?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the bill seemed aimed at increasing election prosecutions, and potentially intimidating voters and local election officials." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/b6087a3c-8aa4-4521-8beb-3c64d3fcbd0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
